projects.title: CRM Digital FTE — Omnichannel AI Customer Success Agent
projects.context: Panaversity Hackathon 5 — CRM Digital FTE Factory Program. Built a production-grade autonomous AI agent to replace a $75,000/year human customer success role with a system costing under $1,000/year.
projects.description: Autonomous AI customer success agent handling Gmail, WhatsApp, and Web Form simultaneously — 24/7, no human in the loop. Built with OpenAI Agents SDK (Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B), FastAPI, Neon PostgreSQL + pgvector for semantic search, Apache Kafka for event streaming, and deployed on Hugging Face Spaces + Vercel.
projects.problem: A SaaS company needs 24/7 customer support across 3 channels simultaneously. A human FTE costs $75,000/year, works 40 hours/week, handles one channel at a time, and can't maintain consistent tone at 3 AM. The company also needs every interaction logged, escalated intelligently, and searchable — none of which a human naturally produces.
projects.solution: A single AI agent receives every inbound message (Email via Gmail Pub/Sub, WhatsApp via Twilio, Web Form via Next.js), normalizes it into one Kafka event, then runs 5 OpenAI Agents SDK tools in sequence: create_ticket → get_customer_history → search_knowledge_base (pgvector cosine similarity) → send_response (or escalate_to_human). Sentiment guardrails auto-detect 5 escalation triggers before the LLM even runs. Total operating cost: ~$1,000/year.

gallery.caption: Web Support Form — glassmorphism UI (Next.js 15 + Tailwind, deployed on Vercel)
Form filled — Technical Support ticket, High priority, real submission
Ticket submitted — AI agent queued, ticket ID returned in < 200ms
Swagger UI — 6 live endpoints across Email, WhatsApp, and Web Form channels
Health check — local_queue mode, all 3 channels active on Hugging Face Spaces
GitHub repo — full source: FastAPI backend, Next.js frontend, Kubernetes manifests, 45 tests

metrics: 3 channels handled simultaneously — Email, WhatsApp, Web Form — from a single agent
< 3s P95 response time via Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B (vs 2–5 min human first-response)
98.7% cost reduction — ~$1,000/year AI operating cost vs $75,000/year human FTE
45/45 tests passing — 18 agent guardrail, 21 channel formatter, 6 end-to-end
8-table Neon PostgreSQL schema with 384-dim pgvector embeddings for semantic KB search
5 escalation triggers: pricing, refund, legal threat, profanity, human request
highlights: USE_LOCAL_QUEUE toggle: same codebase runs on Apache Kafka (production) or asyncio.Queue (HF Spaces) — no Java needed for demo deployment
MCP Server (FastMCP) exposes all 5 agent tools as a Model Context Protocol server — connect any MCP-compatible host (Claude Desktop, Cursor)
Cross-channel customer identity: same customer tracked across Email, WhatsApp, and Web Form via unified customer_identifiers table
Sentiment guardrails run before the LLM — 5 keyword buckets (pricing, refund, legal, aggressive, human_requested) escalate deterministically in O(1)
pgvector cosine similarity search with all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384-dim) + ILIKE fallback when vector search returns no results
Gmail credentials injected from HF Space secrets at container startup — zero credentials in the Docker image
Kubernetes manifests included with HPA auto-scaling (3–30 pods) for production load

projects.title: Todo App → Cloud-Native AI Agent (Spec-Driven Evolution)
projects.context: Panaversity Hackathon II submission. Goal: master spec-driven development by evolving a simple CLI app into a fully distributed, event-driven, Kubernetes-deployed AI agent. Zero manual coding — every line generated by Claude Code from written specs.
projects.description: 5-phase evolution from a simple Python CLI todo list to a full cloud-native AI agent on Kubernetes. Demonstrates spec-driven development methodology from first principles to production.
projects.problem: Show a complete software evolution journey — from console CLI to AI-powered app to Kubernetes-deployed microservices, built entirely through spec-driven development with Claude Code.
projects.solution: 5 progressive phases: CLI → Web App → AI Chatbot (MCP + OpenAI Agents SDK) → Local Kubernetes (Minikube + Helm) → Kafka + Dapr event-driven architecture with production backend on Hugging Face Spaces.

gallery.caption: Hero — AI-powered task management landing page
Features — AI Chatbot, MCP Integration, JWT Auth, Roman Urdu support
Auth — Better Auth JWT login with gradient UI
Register — Account creation with Better Auth

phases.title: Python CLI Console App
phases.description: In-memory todo app with Add, Delete, Update, View, Mark Complete. Built with 100% spec-driven development — no manual code. Spec written first, Claude Code generates everything.

phases.title: Full-Stack Web Application
phases.tech: Next.js 16, FastAPI, SQLModel, Neon PostgreSQL, Better Auth
phases.description: Multi-user web app with JWT authentication (Better Auth), RESTful API with 6 endpoints, persistent Neon Serverless PostgreSQL database, and responsive Tailwind UI.

phases.title: AI Chatbot Interface
phases.tech: OpenAI Agents SDK, Official MCP SDK, OpenAI ChatKit
phases.description: Natural language task management — just say 'Reschedule my morning meetings to 2 PM.' MCP server exposes 5 tools (add_task, list_tasks, complete_task, delete_task, update_task). Stateless server architecture with DB-persisted conversation state.

phases.title: Local Kubernetes Deployment
phases.tech: Docker, Minikube, Helm Charts, kubectl-ai, Kagent, Gordon
phases.description: Containerized frontend and backend. Helm charts generated via AI (kubectl-ai). Gordon (Docker AI Agent) for AI-assisted Docker operations. AIOps with kubectl-ai for intelligent cluster management.

phases.title: Advanced Cloud Deployment
phases.tech: Kafka, Dapr, Redpanda, Hugging Face Spaces
phases.description: Event-driven architecture with 3 Kafka topics (task-events, reminders, task-updates). Dapr sidecars abstract Kafka, DB, and secrets. Recurring task engine and real-time multi-client sync. Production backend on Hugging Face Spaces; local Kubernetes via Minikube + Helm.

metrics: 5 complete evolution phases — CLI to Cloud in one project
Natural language task management via MCP + Agents SDK
Local Kubernetes on Minikube with Helm; production backend on Hugging Face Spaces
Vercel auto-deploy on push to main; backend deployed via HF Space
highlights: 100% spec-driven — every line written by Claude Code, no manual coding
MCP server with 5 tools: add, list, complete, delete, update tasks
Stateless server architecture — horizontally scalable, resilient to restarts
Kafka event-driven: reminder engine, recurring tasks, real-time multi-client sync
Dapr sidecar pattern: swap Kafka/DB without touching application code
Real-time browser notifications — in-app toast + OS push on reminder tasks, powered by DB polling
Roman Urdu chatbot support for multilingual workflows

projects.title: Personal AI Employee — Autonomous Digital FTE
projects.context: A personal project to replace a $4,000–$8,000/month virtual assistant with a 24/7 autonomous AI agent costing $500–$2,000/month. Built across 4 progressive tiers — Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum — each adding a new layer of autonomy.
projects.description: A local-first autonomous AI agent that acts as a Digital Full-Time Employee. It monitors Gmail and WhatsApp, triages emails, drafts Odoo invoices, publishes social media posts, and generates weekly CEO briefings — all autonomously, with Human-in-the-Loop approval gates before any irreversible action.
projects.problem: Repetitive business tasks (email triage, invoice creation, social media posting, weekly reporting) consume 10–15 hours/week. A human VA costs $4,000–$8,000/month, works 40 hours/week, and takes days off. The tasks are rule-based enough to automate — but sensitive enough to require human oversight before execution.
projects.solution: A 4-tier autonomous agent system built on an Obsidian vault as the source of truth. Tasks flow through 9 folders (Inbox → Needs_Action → In_Progress → Pending_Approval → Approved → Done). The agent claims tasks atomically via os.rename(), processes them with Groq + Claude, then halts at Pending_Approval for human sign-off before any irreversible action — email send, invoice confirm, social post publish.

gallery.caption: LinkedIn post published live — 'Post successful!' confirmation after autonomous Playwright execution
Live post on Murad's LinkedIn profile — agent-drafted and published without manual intervention
Agent drafting the post — Playwright session active, content reviewed before publish
HITL approval gate — $3,500 invoice drafted in Odoo, awaiting human approval in Pending_Approval/
Monday Morning CEO Briefing — auto-generated: pipeline metrics, channel status, system health across all 4 tiers
GitHub repo — 4-tier architecture: Bronze → Silver → Gold → Platinum, fully documented

projects.demo_note: Local-first deployment — no public URL. GitHub repo + live proof screenshots above.
metrics: 168 hours/week uptime (24/7) vs 40 hours for a human VA
85–90% cost reduction — $500–2,000/month vs $4,000–8,000/month human VA
4 tiers completed: Bronze (Vault) → Silver (Gmail) → Gold (Odoo + Social) → Platinum (Cloud Brain)
22 emails auto-triaged in one week; 10 tasks completed autonomously
LinkedIn post published live via Playwright — zero manual steps
Atomic task claiming via os.rename() — safe multi-agent coordination without a message broker
highlights: 9-folder Obsidian vault pipeline: every task flows Inbox → Needs_Action → In_Progress → Pending_Approval → Done — full audit trail in plain Markdown
HITL approval gate: agent halts at Pending_Approval/ before any irreversible action — email send, invoice confirm, social post publish
Dual-brain architecture: Groq cloud (llama-3.1-8b-instant) for fast triage + Claude local (claude-sonnet-4-6) for complex reasoning
Playwright social automation: agent opens LinkedIn/X/Facebook sessions, drafts posts, and publishes — proved live with real posts
Odoo XML-RPC integration: invoice drafted automatically from a natural language email request ($3,500 Ahmad Ali — Development Services)
Monday Morning CEO Briefing: auto-generated every week — pipeline metrics, email counts, financial overview, system health across all 4 tiers

projects.title: Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics Textbook
projects.context: Panaversity Hackathon I submission — 100 base points + 150 bonus points. Goal: build a production-grade AI-native textbook for the Physical AI & Humanoid Robotics course. Top submissions were invited to join the Panaversity core team as startup founders.
projects.description: Panaversity Hackathon I submission: a production-grade AI-native textbook platform teaching Physical AI and Humanoid Robotics across 13 weeks. Covers ROS 2, Gazebo, NVIDIA Isaac Sim, and a Conversational Humanoid capstone. Built with Docusaurus v3, FastAPI, Neon PostgreSQL, Qdrant Cloud, LangChain, and Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B.
projects.problem: Physical AI — AI systems that operate in the real physical world — is the next frontier after LLMs, yet no structured, accessible textbook exists for it. Existing robotics courses are scattered, assume identical hardware for every learner, and offer zero in-context AI help when a student gets stuck. Pakistani learners face an additional barrier: dense English-only material with no mechanism to get real-time clarification in their native language.
projects.solution: A full-stack AI-native textbook platform with 4 progressive modules (ROS 2 → Gazebo → NVIDIA Isaac → VLA Capstone). RAG chatbot built with LangChain + Qdrant + Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B — highlight any text → Ask AI instantly. Hardware-aware personalization across 3 rig profiles. One-click Roman Urdu chapter translation.

gallery.caption: Home — Full platform overview with core technologies and stats
Hero — 13-week program, 50+ lessons, Edge AI + RAG + Sim-to-Real
Auth gate — Hardware-aware onboarding with RTX / Jetson / Unitree G1 profiles

modules.title: The Robotic Nervous System (ROS 2)
modules.description: ROS 2 architecture, nodes, topics, services, and actions. Building ROS 2 packages with Python. URDF robot description format for humanoids. Bridging Python AI agents to ROS controllers via rclpy.

modules.title: The Digital Twin (Gazebo & Unity)
modules.description: Physics simulation with gravity, collisions, and rigid body dynamics. Simulating sensors: LiDAR, depth cameras, and IMUs. High-fidelity rendering and human-robot interaction in Unity.

modules.title: The AI-Robot Brain (NVIDIA Isaac)
modules.description: NVIDIA Isaac Sim for photorealistic simulation and synthetic data generation. Isaac ROS with hardware-accelerated VSLAM. Nav2 path planning for bipedal movement. Sim-to-real RL transfer techniques.

modules.title: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) Capstone
modules.description: Integrating LLMs for natural language robot control. Voice commands → ROS 2 action sequences. Capstone: a simulated humanoid receives a voice command, plans a path, navigates obstacles, identifies an object using computer vision, and manipulates it.

metrics: 13-week curriculum across 4 modules (ROS 2 → Gazebo → Isaac → VLA)
180 vectors indexed in Qdrant Cloud for RAG across all 13 weeks
12-table Neon PostgreSQL schema (users, profiles, sessions, curriculum)
3 hardware profiles with fully personalized content paths
All hackathon bonus deliverables: Auth + Personalization + Urdu Translation
highlights: RAG chatbot: highlight any text → Ask AI for instant clarification (LangChain + Qdrant + Groq LLaMA 3.3 70B)
Hardware-aware onboarding: content adapts to RTX workstation, Jetson edge kit, or Unitree G1 robot
One-click Roman Urdu translation for every chapter — built for Pakistani learners
Admin dashboard with curriculum ingestion pipeline and Qdrant reindexing
Full CI/CD: GitHub Actions auto-deploys to GitHub Pages on every push
